channel.title: Requesting Greenlight for SCP (Your Chance to Win)
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Requesting Greenlight for SCP (Your Chance to Win)" - A gameshow host with a twist.

item.title: Re: Requesting Greenlight for SCP (Your Chance to Win)
item.content:encoded:
All-in-all, this seems really predictable. It really just operates like a normal quiz show, outside of the petty vandalism. The anomalies don't add anything interesting to that baseline.

With these kind of SCPs, it can often help to think about the "personality" of the SCP, or at least what it "wants." On this read-through, it looks like they just want to run a normal quiz show. You could dig further into that, push what this being/operation is, what they want, and how they try to get it. There are a lot of directions you could go from there (making them sympathetic, making them really alien, etc.), but it'll take a lot of further development for this to go anywhere.

Compare this to something like SCP-2030, which only has the basic form of a TV show. (There's a really good breakdown of what makes SCP-2030 unique and effective in this post)

item.title: Requesting Greenlight for SCP (Your Chance to Win)
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Genre (Optional): Semi-horror

Page Layout (Optional): SCP Class (Euclid), Containment Protocol, Description, (Possibly Picture)

Elevator Pitch: SCP-XXXX is a radio tower built somewhere around the 1980s around Ballard, Utah. It has not been fed power since its abandonment somewhere between 1985 and 1990. The tower broadcasts a radio signal which is still able to be picked up by radio receivers approximately six miles in the surrounding area.

Central Narrative: Every three to four days, SCP-XXXX broadcasts a signal in which a excited voice is heard announcing something (email, last few credit card numbers, social security number, home address, license etc.) to identify a seemingly random individual. Other than these occurances, only static can be heard when tuned to the channel. (This is bizarre due to the lack of power being sent to the tower)

Hook/Attention-Grabber: Within the least recorded timeframe of a few hours to the most of roughly a year, an entity (deemed SCP-XXXX-2) visits the individual previously announced on the radio transmission. This entity is dressed in a formal attire with a black suit, pants, and shoes with a white undershirt and purple tie. SCP-XXXX-2 tells the person that he is from a reward group and that the person has been chosen to have a chance at winning "glamorous prizes" and requests to sit down with the individual. In the case that the subject accepts, SCP-XXXX-2 expresses apparent gratitude and sits down with them. Once seated, he asks the subject questions ranging from trivia, to multiple choice. If the subject answers correctly, SCP-XXXX-2 leaves their sight and disappears. The following day, the subject receives gifts such as new kitchen equipment, cars, furniture, decor or gift cards. In the case that the subject declines, SCP-XXXX-2 shows frustrating, discomfort and disdain towards the subject. As the same as the previous case, leaves the subject's sight and disappears. The exception to this case is that immediately (30 seconds) to later (days to weeks) something damaging happens to the subject's house. (chandeliers coming loose and falling, to kidnapping/home invasion)

Additional Notes: Please rate the professionality of the article and the overall idea. Thank you.
